skill.name: nano-banana
skill.description: Generate and edit images with Google Gemini native image models through the inference.sh CLI. Supports text prompts, image inputs, aspect ratios, resolution options, and Google Search grounding.
skill.summary: Run Gemini native image generation and image editing through inference.sh CLI commands.
skill.author: inferen-sh

security_audit.summary: Static command and network findings are mostly true positives for documented inference.sh CLI usage, but they match the stated image-generation purpose. The weak cryptography alerts are false positives from ordinary words such as description and design, with no cryptographic code found. No prompt injection, credential harvesting, destructive commands, or hidden exfiltration logic was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Required
medium_findings.description: The skill grants Bash access for infsh commands and documents multiple infsh app run workflows. This is expected for the skill purpose, but it executes a local CLI that sends prompts and image inputs to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed tool declaration and command examples directly show infsh execution. The behavior is legitimate for the skill, but it still creates an external-command risk surface.

medium_findings.title: External Network Processing of Prompts and Images
medium_findings.description: The skill is designed to use inference.sh and Google Gemini image apps, including optional remote image URLs and Google Search grounding. Users should treat prompts, source images, and generated outputs as data sent to third-party services.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly references inference.sh, remote image input, and Google Search grounding. This confirms network processing, although it is aligned with the advertised capability.

low_findings.title: Documented External Skill Installation Commands
low_findings.description: The skill recommends npx skills add commands for related skills. These are documentation examples rather than automatic execution, but users should review external packages before installing them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The npx commands are visible in documentation and are not hidden or automatically invoked. The supply-chain concern is real but user-mediated.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Static Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography findings point to prose and parameter-table text, not cryptographic functions or algorithms. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or similar weak cryptographic use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain natural-language description, a logo design prompt, and a table header. There is no executable cryptographic code at those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash Tool Allowlist for infsh Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The allowed-tools field permits Bash execution of infsh commands. This is scoped to the expected CLI but still permits networked model runs through that tool.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The permission is explicit in the skill header. The command namespace is constrained to infsh, reducing but not eliminating risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: Remote URL Image Input
dangerous_patterns.description: The image editing example passes a remote image URL to the model input. This can expose source-image locations and content to external processing.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The example explicitly shows an images array containing a URL. The privacy concern depends on what image URL the user supplies.

content.value_statement: AI image work often requires switching between prompts, model choices, and command syntax. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused workflow for Gemini image generation through inference.sh.
actual_capabilities: Runs Gemini 3 Pro Image and Gemini 2.5 Flash Image apps through infsh.
Creates images from text prompts with optional style, lighting, and composition details.
Edits existing images by passing image inputs and edit instructions.
Supports multi-image generation with the num_images option.
Shows aspect ratio, resolution, output format, and Google Search grounding options.
Provides sample commands for listing apps and generating input templates.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI to be installed and authenticated.
Sends prompts and image inputs to external model services.
Does not include local image generation or offline processing.
Model availability, cost, and output quality depend on inference.sh and Google services.

use_cases.title: Create Campaign Visuals
use_cases.description: Generate multiple concept images from a short creative brief, then refine the prompt for final assets.

use_cases.title: Prototype Product Imagery
use_cases.description: Create product mockups, style variants, and aspect-ratio options from plain-language prompts.

use_cases.title: Edit Reference Images
use_cases.description: Use an existing image as input and request targeted changes such as added elements or new background details.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Text Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a single generated image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana to generate one image of [subject]. Use a [style] style, [lighting] lighting, and a [aspect ratio] format.

prompt_templates.title: Multiple Concepts
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate prompt for exploring visual options.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana to create four concept images for [project]. Compare Gemini 2.5 Flash Image speed with Gemini 3 Pro Image quality.

prompt_templates.title: Edit Existing Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana to edit this image: [image URL]. Change [specific element], preserve [details], and return a concise output summary.

prompt_templates.title: Grounded Visual Brief
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt using current information and production constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use nano-banana with Google Search grounding to create a visual scene about [current topic]. Use Gemini 3 Pro Image, 4K resolution, and [aspect ratio].

output_examples.input: Create a photorealistic square image of a banana floating above a reflective table.
output_examples.output: The skill prepares an infsh Gemini image request and returns generated image results with a short note about the model used.

output_examples.input: Edit a landscape image by adding a rainbow and preserving the mountains.
output_examples.output: The skill sends the image input and edit prompt to Gemini through inference.sh, then reports the generated edited image output.

output_examples.input: Generate four logo concepts for a coffee shop in a minimalist style.
output_examples.output: The skill requests multiple images from Gemini 2.5 Flash Image and summarizes the returned concept set.

best_practices: Use specific prompts that name subject, style, composition, lighting, and output ratio.
Avoid sending private images, confidential brand assets, or sensitive prompts to external services.
Start with Gemini 2.5 Flash Image for drafts, then use Gemini 3 Pro Image for final quality.
anti_patterns: Do not assume generated images are rights-cleared for every commercial use.
Do not include secrets, credentials, or private customer data in prompts or image URLs.
Do not install related skills or CLI tools without reviewing their source and permissions.

faq.question: What does this skill do?
faq.answer: It helps an AI assistant run inference.sh commands for Gemini native image generation and image editing.

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it work offline?
faq.answer: No. It requires the inference.sh CLI and external model services.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing image?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill documents image input support for editing tasks.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill intentionally runs a CLI and sends data to network services, which requires user awareness.

faq.question: Were malicious instructions found?
faq.answer: No evidence found of prompt injection, hidden exfiltration, destructive commands, or credential theft.
